svg.text: SochDB v0.5.0 — Complete Architecture & Data Flow
Rust gRPC Vector Database with HNSW Indexing · SIMD-Accelerated · Deferred Indexing
• search(query, k, ef=0) ← CHANGED: was ef=50
max_decoding_message_size = 64 MB · max_encoding_message_size = 64 MB · Metrics: port 9090 · WS: port 8080
search_with_ef delegates to flat scan for <50K
Scaffold: first 2×M₀ = 64 nodes inserted sequentially (full HNSW connections)
Triggered on FIRST SEARCH after insert batch
search_with_ef(q, k, ef) → delegates to flat scan for ≤50K (ignores ef)
Ensures all search paths benefit from exact brute-force on small datasets
Pre-normalized at ingest for cosine metric
Batch extend under ONE lock (minimize contention)
❺ CRITICAL CHANGES — From Broken to #1
insert_batch_flat() called rebuild_layer0_exact() after every batch
O(N²×D) operation — destroyed good HNSW graphs, killed performance
gRPC create_index used ef_search=128, far too low for high recall
grpc_client.py hardcoded ef=50 in search requests
insert_batch_flat() only stores vectors + defers connections
Connections built lazily on first search via build_pending_connections()
Optimal balance of recall vs latency for benchmark workloads
ef=0 means "use server index default" — no client override
New: Flat Scan for ≤50K vectors (brute-force, exact)
New: SIMD dot_product_fast for normalized vectors
MicroK8s available but direct binary used for benchmarks
Rust edition 2021 · Dependencies: dashmap, rayon, parking_lot, tonic
SochDB v0.5.0 Architecture Diagram · All metrics: #1 vs Qdrant v1.17.1
